message:
test(connectors,core,bootstrap): propagate C3.4 constraints + BLAKE3 prefix to remaining test tokens and vectors

Follow-up catch-up for two recent initiatives that left a handful of
test fixtures/vectors trailing:

1. C3.4 default-deny CapabilityConstraints propagation
   (extends prior 96b66ad9 and fe19d330 which added constraints to 43
    connector test tokens; this finishes the sweep to the last 9 + 2
    regenerated golden vectors)

   - connectors/{anthropic,discord,gmail,github,openai,slack,stripe,
     telegram}/src/connector.rs: add CapabilityConstraints {
     resource_allow: ["*"], .. } and pipe through .constraints_cbor(&cbor)
     in every test-token builder
   - discord additionally switches CapabilityArtifact { raw: cose } to
     CapabilityArtifact::from_raw(cose) for API hygiene
   - connectors/google-docs/src/connector.rs: replace
     configured.store(true, Ordering::Relaxed) with set_configured(true)
     (encapsulation cleanup)
   - crates/fcp-core/tests/vectors/enrollment/request_vectors.cbor
   - crates/fcp-core/tests/vectors/simulate/simulate_request_vectors.cbor
     (both regenerated to include the default-deny constraints block)

2. BLAKE3: fingerprint prefix catch-up
   (follows 162a3e71 which renamed the human-readable prefix to match
    the actual blake3 hash function)

   - crates/fcp-bootstrap/tests/vectors/expected_fingerprint.txt:
     SHA256:8lDKED5d4i5sbxu0... → BLAKE3:8lDKED5d4i5sbxu0...

No behavioral change — test-token schemas now match the spec (C3.4)
and fingerprint label matches the hash algorithm (BLAKE3).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
